input: I failed my driving test twice.
name: Joshua Fong
headline: Visionary in Motion | Navigating Life's Detours | ex-McKinsey | Resilience Coach | Dog Dad
post_body:
I failed.

Not once.

Twice.

The examiner looked at me with that specific kind of pity reserved for people who indicate left and turn right.

And in that moment, I realised something.

Failure is not the opposite of success.

It is the curriculum.

Every missed mirror check was feedback.
Every emergency stop was a masterclass in presence.

I passed on my third attempt.

But more importantly — I passed on my own terms.

If the road feels long, maybe you're just not in the right lane yet.

comments.name: Marcus Webb
comments.title: Growth Lead | Navigating Complexity | ex-Uber | Building in Public
comments.comment: This is exactly why resilience is the new KPI. The third attempt is where character is built. 🙌

comments.name: Fatima Al-Hassan
comments.title: 2x Founder | Strategic Advisor | LinkedIn Top Voice 2024
comments.comment: Needed this today. The lane metaphor is everything.

comments.name: Daniel Park
comments.title: Senior Strategy Lead | Change Maker | Keynote Speaker
comments.comment: Failure as curriculum — I'm putting this on our team's Notion page. Real leadership energy here. 💡

input: I got rejected from my dream job.
name: Sarah Chen
headline: Chief Possibility Officer | Rewriting Rejection | ex-Goldman | Purpose-Led Leader | Mom of 3
post_body:
They said no.

A two-line email.

No feedback. No call.

Just: "We've decided to move forward with other candidates."

I sat with that for exactly 11 minutes.

Then I opened my laptop.

Because here's what they didn't know:

Every closed door is a redirect, not a dead end.

Six months later I built the company that company was trying to hire me to fix.

Rejection didn't break me.

It gave me the brief.

Your dream job might just be the one you haven't invented yet.

comments.name: Lena Kovács
comments.title: Head of People | Culture Architect | ex-Stripe | She/Her
comments.comment: "Rejection gave me the brief" — screenshot'd and sent to my entire network. This is the content LinkedIn was made for. 🔥

comments.name: Tom Okafor
comments.title: 3x Founder | Operator | Investor | Building What's Next
comments.comment: The pivot from rejection to founder is a story I see in every great builder. Powerful share, Sarah.

comments.name: Yuki Tanaka
comments.title: Senior Innovation Lead | Future of Work Enthusiast | LinkedIn Top Voice 2023
comments.comment: This resonates so deeply. Closed doors really are just GPS recalculating. 🙏

input: My flight got cancelled and I missed an important meeting.
name: James Oduya
headline: Executive in Transit | Mastering the Uncontrollable | ex-Deloitte | Systems Thinker | Frequent Flyer
post_body:
Gate B14.

Delayed. Then cancelled.

The meeting I'd prepared three weeks for — gone.

I watched grown adults argue with airport staff about weather.

And I thought: this is the whole game, isn't it.

You can't control the fog.

You can only control how you move through it.

I called the client from a café next to a Pret A Manger.

No slides. No deck. Just clarity.

We closed the deal.

Sometimes the cancelled flight is the pitch.

The best leaders I know aren't prepared for everything.

They're comfortable with anything.

comments.name: Raj Mehta
comments.title: Principal at Scale | Operations Excellence | ex-BCG | Advisor
comments.comment: "Comfortable with anything" is a leadership superpower that can't be taught in an MBA. 🎯

comments.name: Sofia Bergström
comments.title: Chief of Staff | Executive Coach | Building Calm Leaders
comments.comment: The Pret A Manger detail is doing a lot of work here and I am here for it. Brilliant post.

comments.name: Kevin Asante
comments.title: VP Growth | Revenue Obsessed | 2x Exit | Now Advising
comments.comment: Closed a deal from Gate B14 energy is the vibe we all need this quarter honestly. ✈️
